commit.message:
Fix timeline sort bug causing spending tracker events to be skipped

The sortEvents() method used dayjs.diff('day') which truncates sub-day
differences, making events 12 hours apart (e.g. Oct 31 noon vs Nov 1
midnight) appear as same-day. This caused the linear segment scanner
to skip spending tracker events when they sorted after next-day events
due to priority ordering, permanently dropping them from calculation.

Fix: use millisecond-precision getTime() comparison instead of dayjs diff.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
week02 slide 5: add vocal fold animation sub-slide (CSS d:path)

Add Slide 5b 'Vocal Fold Movement' as a vertical sub-slide below
the static Voicing diagram. Inline SVG uses CSS @keyframes with
d:path() to animate the voiced ↔ voiceless cycle in a 6-second loop:
- Voiced phase (0–40 %): three vibration oscillations, ≈ markers visible
- Opening transition (40–55 %)
- Voiceless phase (55–75 %): folds abducted, airflow arrow visible
- Closing transition (75–90 %)
Background colour, state labels and ≈ / airflow elements all cross-fade
in sync.  No external resources required; scrollH ≤ 700 px on both slides.

commit.message:
Add level system with spaced repetition and AI chat tutor

Replace flat category-based flashcards with 8-level progression
toward A0 German proficiency (~86 vocabulary items). Features:

- Level map with locked/current/completed states
- Flashcard study sessions with new + review cards (spaced repetition)
- 4-option multiple choice quizzes with 80% pass threshold
- localStorage persistence for progress and per-word statistics
- Review cards prioritize weakest words (fewest correct, oldest seen)
- AI chat tutor (Claude API) with context about learning progress
- Full keyboard support (Space, arrows, 1-4 keys)
- Responsive design (quiz grid stacks on mobile)

commit.message:
Initial commit: Family Cookbook app prototype

Single-file mobile app prototype with 9 screens: Home, Sign In, Sign Up,
Table of Contents, Recipe, Add Recipe, Family, Profile, and Settings.
Features include editable TOC sections, recipe sharing, and family member management.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix file upload: use client-side Vercel Blob uploads to bypass 4.5MB limit

Vercel serverless functions have a 4.5MB body size limit, causing
"Request Entity Too Large" errors when uploading architectural PDFs.
Files now upload directly from the browser to Vercel Blob via client
upload tokens, then the form sends just text fields + blob URLs as
a small JSON payload to the API.

commit.message:
refactor(scripting): migrate from smol::block_on to promise::spawn::block_on

Replace the external smol crate dependency with the project's own promise
crate for blocking async execution in the Lua scripting engine and config
loader. This reduces external dependency surface and uses the same async
runtime primitives already used elsewhere in the frankenterm codebase.

- config.rs: smol::block_on → promise::spawn::block_on for config loading
- lua.rs: smol::block_on → promise::spawn::block_on in 3 test call sites
- lua_engine.rs: smol::block_on → promise::spawn::block_on in eval/exec
- Cargo.toml (scripting): swap dep:smol for dep:promise in lua feature
- Cargo.lock: updated to reflect dependency change

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(vdbe): revert accidental SharedTxnPageIo concurrent handle changes

The previous commit accidentally included another agent's unfinished
refactor of SharedTxnPageIo::read_page that changed ConcurrentRegistry
from get_mut() to get() with a separate .lock() call. This broke
compilation because ConcurrentHandle does not implement the expected
Mutex-like interface. Revert to the original get_mut() approach.

commit.message:
Pr/html sanitization phase1 (#2)

* Relax HTML sanitization for safer email fidelity

Document the phased sanitization plan and preserve more sender-intended presentation by allowing sanitized inline CSS and common email layout attributes while keeping remote content and active elements blocked.

Co-authored-by: Codex (GPT-5.4) <codex@openai.com>

* Block CSS image fetch functions in sanitizer

Tighten the inline CSS policy by disallowing background shorthand and rejecting image-set/image CSS values so remote resources cannot be fetched through sanitized styles.

Co-authored-by: Codex (GPT-5.4) <codex@openai.com>

* Version sanitized HTML policy and lazy upgrade

Add a persisted sanitized HTML version, migrate existing rows to version 1, and lazily re-sanitize cached message bodies to policy version 2 on first access while keeping the updated sanitizer behavior and docs aligned.

Co-authored-by: Codex (GPT-5.4) <codex@openai.com>

* Harden sanitizer: block svg/math/base, restrict data: to img[src], dedup BuildFallbackHtmlAsync

- Add svg, math, foreignobject, use, base to disallowed elements to close
  XSS vectors from SVG/MathML namespaces and base-tag URL hijacking
- Restrict data: URI allowance to img[src] only, preventing phishing via
  data:text/html on anchor hrefs
- Extract duplicated BuildFallbackHtmlAsync into HtmlSanitizer to prevent
  future divergence between ImapSyncService and McpTools
- Bump CurrentPolicyVersion to 3 to trigger lazy resanitization

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex (GPT-5.4) <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix DOCX parsing: replace broken ZIP reader with fflate library

The custom ZIP reader used DecompressionStream("raw") which is not a
valid format (should be "deflate-raw"), causing all compressed entries
to silently fail. Replaced with fflate via esm.sh — a battle-tested
pure JS zip library that works reliably in Deno.

commit.message:
feat: add smart auto-approve for read-only commands and project-internal edits

Server-side intelligent permission auto-approval that reduces manual
approvals for safe operations:
- Read-only tools (Read, Glob, Grep) auto-approved
- Bash commands from a whitelist (cat, ls, grep, git log, etc.) auto-approved
- Write/Edit within the session's project directory auto-approved
- Dangerous commands (rm, curl, sudo, etc.) always require manual approval

Enabled by default via smart_auto_approve flag.

feat: OMC 분석 기반 3대 강화 — Critic 독립 검증, 병렬 구현, 품질 게이트

## 새 기능
- critic.md: 독립 검증 에이전트 신규 생성 (Architect와 분리된 비판자)
- plan.md Step 5.5: Critic 독립 검증 단계 (80% 파일참조, 90% 테스트가능 기준)
  - --quick: Critic 생략 / --consensus: 최대 3회 Architect↔Critic 루프
- plan.md: 1회 1질문 원칙 (OMC best practice 적용)
- plan.md: squad-plan.json에 병렬 그룹(groups) + 의존성 그래프 스키마 추가
- build.md Phase 3: 독립 그룹 병렬 구현 (run_in_background: true)
  - 동일 파일 충돌 감지 시 순차 폴백

## 개선
- build.md Phase 0: plan-ready 상태 명시적 처리 + 메시지 분기
- analyze.md: guard-plan-mode.sh 참조 → mode: "plan" 으로 갱신

## OMC 대비 Squad 차별점
- 2000줄 경량 구조 유지하면서 OMC의 핵심 3가지(Critic/병렬/품질게이트) 흡수
- OMC에 없는 자기개선 루프(agent-effectiveness, false-positives) 유지
- 컨텍스트 효율성(1000자 요약 원칙, 세션 분리) 유지

commit.message:
fix(frankenterm-core): resolve clippy warnings across benchmarks, core modules, and tests

Systematic cleanup of clippy lints identified during Rust 2024 edition
preparation. No behavioral changes — all fixes are mechanical
transformations that preserve existing semantics.

Source modules:
- command_transport: assert!(x.len() >= 1) → assert!(!x.is_empty())
- connector_credential_broker: numeric literal grouping (3600_000 → 3_600_000)
- connector_data_classification: float equality via f64::EPSILON, allow(similar_names)
- connector_inbound_bridge: float equality via f64::EPSILON
- connector_mesh: float equality via f64::EPSILON
- lru_cache: allow(clippy::len_zero) on invariant checker
- metrics: avoid format_float value that trips float-comparison lint
- ring_buffer, skip_list, swarm_pipeline: minor lint fixes

Benchmarks:
- dashboard_aggregation: mul_add() instead of manual multiply-then-add
- mux_pool_scaling: Box::pin() wrapping for async recursive calls
- native_events_latency: simplify match → while-let for backpressure loop
- tui_rendering, wa_agent_streaming: minor lint fixes

Tests (27 files):
- Unnecessary .clone() on Copy types removed
- &raw → raw for slice references (clippy::needless_borrow)
- map(|_| ()) → map(|()| ()) for unit closures
- fold+Write instead of map+collect for string building
- mut → shared ref where mutation not needed
- Pattern matching simplification (nested OR patterns)
- format!() → .to_string() where applicable

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: normalize subdomain on full Act path (2.4.3)

Act Quick called _normalize_subdomain() but full Act skipped it,
causing alias subdomains like "deals" to fail schema lookup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Cap web searches to 3 and max_tokens to 4000 to reduce API cost

Each web search result accumulates in the context window, causing input tokens
to compound across search rounds (4k → 70k → 134k → 150k in a single run).
Capping at 3 searches prevents runaway context growth. Reducing max_tokens from
8000 to 4000 halves output cost — 4000 tokens is sufficient for a structured report.
Expected cost reduction: ~$0.80 → ~$0.20 per run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(a11y): restore focus-visible ring on NumberInput (WCAG 2.4.11)

focus:outline-none fires on all focus events, including keyboard,
which suppressed the global 3px focus-visible ring defined in
index.css. Changing to focus-visible:outline-none limits suppression
to pointer-initiated focus only, so keyboard users see the ring.

commit.message:
Retry stream.start() not just OutputStream construction

The previous fix only caught PortAudioError during OutputStream(),
but start() is called separately (via __enter__) and can also fail
with the same transient AUHAL error. Now calls start() explicitly
inside the retry loop and manages stop()/close() manually in a
try/finally, so both failure points are covered.
